title: Gabriel Attal wants to reinvent Macronism
subhed: According to his critics, the 37-year-old former prime minister embodies a form of opportunism. While he places himself in the steps of Emmanuel Macron's liberalism, he has set himself apart by making state authority a cornerstone of his politics.

Raphaël Glucksmann draws crowd at rally and takes aim at far right
subhed: The center-left MEP held his first major campaign rally on Saturday, reaffirming his 'total determination' to run for president and relying on the fundamentals that brought him success in the 2024 European elections.

title: 'The AI war has begun': France and Europe worried as US blocks Anthropic's latest AI model
subhed: The American start-up has disabled its latest AI model, Mythos 5, at the request of Washington. From a European perspective, the decision is seen as evidence of US dominance in the sector and the Trump administration's desire for control.

title: The Knicks, the soul of New York, are NBA champions after more than half a century of waiting
subhed: After a long period of anticipation and frustration, New York, where basketball is so much more than just a sport, finally celebrates, following its 94-90 triumph on Saturday in Game 5 of the NBA Finals against Victor Wembanyama's Spurs.

title: At Dior, Jonathan Anderson is thinking long-term, not 'trendy'
subhed: One year after this arrival at Dior, the 41-year-old Northern Irish designer is still going at full throttle, releasing collections for Uniqlo and his own brand JW Anderson in addition to his 10 for the luxury label. His goal, he says, is to 'transform' the brand.

France prepares for another 'very intense and widespread' heat event, possibly over 40°C
subhed: The southern half of France is set to be hit first this weekend. Starting Tuesday, the heat is expected to worsen and move northward. Temperatures could reach or exceed 40°C, although some uncertainty remains.

title: The Algerians who seek peaceful relations with France: 'For my children, I want us to reconcile and move on'
subhed: 'Algeria's unhealed war memories' (2/3). The trauma of independence remains deeply rooted in Algeria, affecting both pro-independence fighters and French conscripts. This fuels demands for recognition of colonial crimes and formal apologies, not just 'transactional diplomacy.'

title: As France faces demographic decline, immigration emerges as both political and economic necessity
subhed: Immigration alone will not be enough to offset France's negative natural population growth in the medium and long term, according to the latest projections from the national statistics institute.
